vocab.meaning: sự đọc kỹ lưỡng, cẩn thận
vocab.example: For academic papers, I opt for meticulous perusal to ensure I don't overlook any critical data.
If I'm just checking the morning news, I usually glance through the headlines to get the gist.

vocab.meaning: trích xuất thông tin chủ chốt
vocab.example: Scanning is my preferred method when I need to extract key information from a dense report in a hurry.

vocab.meaning: đắm chìm vào (một cuốn sách/văn bản)
vocab.example: I love to lose myself in a gripping novel, which naturally requires a more detailed reading approach.

vocab.meaning: sự hiểu biết hời hợt/trên bề mặt
vocab.example: Scanning often only provides a superficial understanding, which isn't enough for complex technical manuals.

vocab.meaning: xem xét/đọc văn bản một cách cực kỳ chi tiết
vocab.example: When reviewing a contract, it is vital to scrutinize the text to avoid any legal pitfalls.
I practice selective reading when I’m researching, focusing only on the chapters relevant to my thesis.
Detailed reading demands a higher cognitive load, so I save it for when I'm most alert.

vocab.meaning: tìm hiểu sâu hơn về cái gì
vocab.example: If a headline catches my eye, I will then dig deeper into the article to understand the underlying causes.

vocab.meaning: tiết kiệm/hiệu quả về mặt thời gian
vocab.example: Scanning is a time-efficient strategy for professionals who have to deal with hundreds of emails daily.
